column1: 1,Cobra Root Command,Use cmd.Execute() as the single entry point,"The root command should call Execute() in main.go and handle the error at the top level, keeping main clean and minimal.",Call rootCmd.Execute() from main and handle errors there,Put business logic directly in main.go or ignore the Execute error,"package main

2,Subcommands,Organize commands in a tree structure,"Group related functionality into subcommands. Place each command in its own file under a cmd/ package to keep things organized and maintainable.",Create separate files per command and add them with AddCommand,Define all commands in a single file or deeply nest unrelated subcommands,"package cmd

3,Persistent Flags,Use PersistentFlags for flags shared across subcommands,"PersistentFlags are inherited by all child commands. Use Flags() for command-specific flags. This avoids duplication and keeps the flag surface consistent.",Use PersistentFlags on the root for global options like --verbose or --config,Use Flags() on root and then manually duplicate them on each subcommand,"package cmd

4,Required Flags,Mark mandatory flags with MarkFlagRequired,"Use MarkFlagRequired so Cobra validates presence before RunE executes. This gives users clear error messages without manual checking.",Call MarkFlagRequired after defining the flag,Manually check for empty flag values inside RunE,"package cmd

5,Viper Config Integration,Bind Cobra flags to Viper with BindPFlags,"Binding flags to Viper allows configuration to come from flags, environment variables, or config files with a single unified API.",Call viper.BindPFlags in an init or PersistentPreRun hook,Read flag values with cmd.Flags().GetString when Viper is also in use,"package cmd

6,Config File Loading,Load config files with viper.ReadInConfig,"Use Viper to load YAML, TOML, or JSON config files. Set the config name and paths, then call ReadInConfig. Handle the file-not-found case gracefully.",Use viper.ReadInConfig and handle ConfigFileNotFoundError as non-fatal,Call os.ReadFile and manually parse JSON/YAML for configuration,"package cmd

7,Environment Variables,Use viper.AutomaticEnv for environment variable support,"AutomaticEnv tells Viper to check environment variables for every key. Use SetEnvPrefix to namespace your vars and SetEnvKeyReplacer for key format conversion.",Call viper.AutomaticEnv with a prefix and replacer,Read os.Getenv manually for each configuration value,"package cmd

8,Shell Completion,Generate shell completion scripts,"Cobra has built-in support for generating bash, zsh, fish, and PowerShell completion scripts. Expose this to users via a completion subcommand.",Use cobra.Command with GenBashCompletionV2 or the built-in completion command,Omit shell completion entirely or write custom completion logic,"package cmd

column3: fmt.Println(""# source this file for completion"")

column1: 9,Args Validation,Validate argument count with Cobra validators,"Use Cobra's built-in argument validators like ExactArgs, MinimumNArgs, MaximumNArgs, and RangeArgs to enforce correct usage before RunE fires.",Set the Args field on your Command struct,Manually check len(args) inside RunE,"package cmd

10,PreRun/PostRun,Use lifecycle hooks for setup and teardown,"Cobra provides PersistentPreRun, PreRun, PostRun, and PersistentPostRun hooks. Use them for initialization, validation, and cleanup instead of mixing that logic into Run.",Use PersistentPreRunE for config loading and PreRunE for command-specific validation,Put setup and teardown logic directly inside RunE alongside business logic,"package cmd

11,Bubbletea TUI,Use Bubbletea for interactive terminal UIs,"Bubbletea implements the Elm architecture for terminal apps. Define a Model with Init, Update, and View methods. Use tea.NewProgram to run it.",Create a proper Model struct implementing tea.Model and run with tea.NewProgram,Use fmt.Scanln loops for interactive input or mix TUI with raw print statements,"package tui
func (m model) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }

12,Lipgloss Styling,Use Lipgloss for styled terminal output,"Lipgloss provides a declarative API for styling terminal strings with colors, borders, padding, and alignment. Define styles as variables and reuse them.",Define reusable lipgloss.Style variables and apply them to output strings,Use raw ANSI escape codes or fmt.Sprintf with hardcoded color codes,"package ui

13,Huh Forms,Use Huh for interactive form input,"Huh provides a declarative API to build multi-step forms with inputs, selects, confirms, and text areas. It integrates with Bubbletea.",Use huh.NewForm with typed field groups for structured input,Use sequential fmt.Scanln calls for multi-field input,"package prompt

14,Progress Bars,Show progress indication for long-running operations,"Use charmbracelet/bubbles progress component inside a Bubbletea program for animated progress bars, or use a simple spinner for indeterminate tasks.",Use bubbles/progress or bubbles/spinner within a tea.Program,Print dots or percentage numbers with fmt.Printf in a loop,"package tui

15,Table Output,Use Lipgloss table for structured terminal output,"Use charmbracelet/lipgloss/table for rendering aligned, styled tables in the terminal. It supports headers, row styling, and border customization.",Use lipgloss table.New with defined columns and rows,Use fmt.Printf with manual spacing or tab characters for alignment,"package ui

16,Color Output,Use terminal colors wisely with automatic detection,"Respect NO_COLOR and TERM environment variables. Use lipgloss.HasDarkBackground for adaptive themes. Never hardcode ANSI codes.",Use lipgloss with environment-aware color profiles,Hardcode ANSI escape sequences or ignore NO_COLOR,"package ui

17,Error Exit Codes,Exit with proper codes for different error types,"Use distinct exit codes: 0 for success, 1 for general errors, 2 for usage errors. Let Cobra handle usage errors and use os.Exit in main only.",Return errors from RunE and map them to exit codes in main,Call os.Exit inside RunE or use log.Fatal in command handlers,"package main

18,Quiet/Verbose Flags,Implement output level control with --quiet and --verbose flags,"Provide --quiet for scripts and --verbose for debugging. Use a shared output helper that respects these flags throughout the application.",Define a logger or writer that checks verbosity level globally via viper,Use if verbose { fmt.Println(...) } scattered throughout the codebase,"package cmd

19,Version Command,Add a --version flag using Cobra's built-in support,"Set the Version field on the root command. Cobra automatically adds a --version flag and version subcommand. Populate the version at build time with ldflags.",Set rootCmd.Version and use ldflags to inject the value at build time,Hardcode a version string or create a manual version subcommand,"package cmd

20,Man Pages,Generate man pages with cobra/doc,"Use the cobra/doc package to generate man pages, markdown docs, and REST documentation from your command tree automatically.",Use doc.GenManTree or doc.GenMarkdownTree in a separate doc generation command,Write man pages by hand or skip documentation generation entirely,"package cmd

21,Testing CLI Commands,Execute commands in tests using the command's ExecuteC method,"Create commands with captured output buffers. Use cmd.SetOut and cmd.SetErr for testable output. Use ExecuteC to run and inspect results.",Use bytes.Buffer with SetOut/SetErr and call ExecuteC in test functions,Test by running os/exec.Command on the compiled binary,"package cmd

22,Stdin/Stdout,Use cmd.InOrStdin and cmd.OutOrStdout for proper I/O,"Cobra commands provide InOrStdin, OutOrStdout, and ErrOrStderr methods. Use them instead of os.Stdin/os.Stdout directly for testability.",Use cmd.OutOrStdout() and cmd.InOrStdin() inside RunE,Use fmt.Println or os.Stdout.Write directly in command handlers,"package cmd

column2: Short: ""Read from stdin and write to stdout"",

column1: 23,Signal Handling,Handle OS signals for graceful shutdown,"Use os/signal.NotifyContext to create a context that cancels on SIGINT or SIGTERM. Pass this context to long-running operations for clean shutdown.",Use signal.NotifyContext for context cancellation on interrupt,Ignore signals or use os.Exit(0) inside a signal handler,"package cmd

column3: fmt.Fprintln(cmd.OutOrStdout(), ""Server starting... Press Ctrl+C to stop."")

column1: 24,Config Precedence,Enforce precedence: flags > env > config file > defaults,"Viper automatically handles precedence when configured correctly. Bind flags with BindPFlags and set defaults with SetDefault. The order is flag > env > config > default.",Bind all flags with viper.BindPFlags and set defaults with viper.SetDefault,Manually check each source and write custom precedence logic,"package cmd

25,Distribution,Use GoReleaser for cross-platform distribution,"GoReleaser automates building, packaging, and publishing Go binaries. Configure .goreleaser.yaml with ldflags to inject version info at build time.",Configure .goreleaser.yaml with ldflags and use goreleaser release in CI,Build manually with go build for each platform or distribute without checksums,"// .goreleaser.yaml
